content: The orthographic evolution of the word "reflexion" (an older variant spelling that gradually shifted to the modern "reflection") can serve as a structural metaphor for how LLM self-reflection mechanisms iteratively refine their outputs. Just as the spelling of the term adjusted over time to align with common linguistic convention based on accumulated usage feedback, LLM self-reflection can be framed as a process of adjusting its own reasoning outputs to align with implicit problem-solving norms, using inconsistencies between multiple perspectives as its "usage feedback" to converge on better solutions.
